title: leontopodium leontopodinum
intro: Leontopodium leontopodinum, also known as Edelweiss, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family and is native to the European Alps and the Himalayas. The medicinal properties of Edelweiss include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to the presence of saponins and flavonoids in its flowers and leaves. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as digestive problems, and its extracts have been shown to exhibit immunomodulatory and antimicrobial effects. Edelweiss is often prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and it can also be used topically to treat wounds and skin irritations.
